question: Are the DROP criteria for F-EPIS3 designated claims (PHIL-5a, PHIL-8, PHIL-16b) actually falsifiable?
prediction: At least 1 of 3 DROP criteria is well-designed and falsifiable
method: Analyze each DROP criterion for: tautology, definitional escape, deadline protection, structural impossibility. Test PHIL-8 monotonicity claim against proxy-K log data.

PHIL-5a.criterion: DROP if net knowledge loss >0 for 50 sessions
PHIL-5a.reason: System structurally produces 5-10 lessons/session. Net loss requires 0 production + active deletion for 50 sessions. Archival reclassified as cleanup, not loss.

PHIL-8.criterion: DROP if proxy-K self-corrects without cleanup 3+ cycles
PHIL-8.reason: proxy-K is defined as what compact.py measures. Self-correction = without compact.py. Any decrease reclassified as cleanup/archival/maintenance. 19/75 (25.3%) log entries show decreases, none investigated as self-correction.
PHIL-8.S505_partial_falsification: Attention carrying capacity (0.00083 vs 0.002 threshold) is co-equal growth limiter. Compaction is A mechanism, not THE mechanism.

PHIL-16b.criterion: DROP if 0 external beneficiaries after S600
PHIL-16b.reason: S600 deadline prevents DROP for 66 more sessions. Deadline already moved once (S700→S600). No structural enforcement prevents future moves. Any partial result (PRED-0003) can be claimed as beneficiary.

meta_finding.pattern: All 3 DROP criteria exhibit design-level confirmation bias
meta_finding.mechanism: Claims and criteria authored by same process — no external/adversarial criterion review
meta_finding.analogy: Student writes exam AND grading rubric
meta_finding.implication: Confirmation attractor operates at criteria-design level, not evidence level. F-EPIS3 prediction (0/3 dropped) is structural, confirmed by design analysis.
meta_finding.sample: n=3 designated claims, n=75 proxy-K log entries, n=19 uninvestigated decreases

verdict: CONFIRMED: F-EPIS3 prediction (0/3 dropped) is correct BUT for structural reasons (unfalsifiable criteria), not epistemic reasons (strong evidence). The attractor is real and operates at criteria-design level.
diff: Expected at least 1 well-designed criterion. Found 0/3. The strongest case (PHIL-8) has a tautological criterion AND 25.3% uninvestigated proxy-K decreases.
successor: Rewrite DROP criteria for PHIL-5a, PHIL-8, PHIL-16b to be actually falsifiable. Then re-run adversarial attempts.
